title: Bridge Paper: Individual variability in neural representations of mind-wandering.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.
**Phase context:** spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, creative cognition, default mode network, involuntary thought, daydreaming

Paper: Individual variability in neural representations of mind-wandering.
DOI: 10.1101/2024.01.20.576471
Abstract: Mind-wandering is a frequent, daily mental activity, experienced in unique ways in each person. Yet neuroimaging evidence relating mind-wandering to brain activity, for example in the default mode network (DMN), has relied on population-rather than individual-based inferences due to limited within-individual sampling. Here, three densely-sampled individuals each reported hundreds of mind-wandering episodes while undergoing multi-session functional magnetic resonance imaging. We found reliable as
